_description: Project-level architectural rules. Each rule is a named check run by release-audit-design.sh against the slice's diff. Rules can be suppressed per-release via architecture-overrides.json. Check types: grep (regex), touchpoints (planned-file audit), diff-size (growth limits), external (SAST/lint tool invocation).

canonical_docs._description: Canonical architectural source-of-truth documents. The planner consults these during discovery. The architecture audit checks conformance. A project without canonical docs is a project where every slice invents its own patterns — the accumulated divergence is exponentially expensive to fix.

rules.description: Files changed shall be declared in spec.md planned touchpoints
rules.note: If intentional, add to spec.md planned touchpoints or declare in proof.md divergence

rules.description: No single file shall grow by more than 200 lines in one slice
rules.note: Large additions may indicate monolithic code dumps — split into smaller composable units

rules.description: No file shall exceed 500 lines
rules.note: Files over 500 lines should be decomposed

rules.description: Financial calculations shall use Go/shopspring-decimal, not TypeScript Decimal.js
rules.note: All financial math must happen in Go using shopspring/decimal. TypeScript displays results only.

rules.description: Financial values in Go shall use decimal.Decimal, not float64 for arithmetic
rules.note: float64 is acceptable for loop counters, array indices, and JSON serialization only. Verify context.

rules.description: New dependencies shall be accompanied by an ADR or justified in proof.md
rules.note: New import or dependency detected. Confirm it is intentional and justified in the proof bundle.

rules.description: API handlers shall use auth middleware — no unauthenticated routes outside /health
rules.note: New route detected. Verify it uses the appropriate auth middleware (JWTAuth or AnonAuth).

rules.description: No secrets, keys, or credentials shall appear in source code
rules.note: Secrets detected in diff. Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.description: SQL queries shall use parameterised queries, not string concatenation
rules.note: SQL injection risk. Use parameterised queries ($1, $2) with pgx.

rules.description: Goroutines shall have cancellation — missing defer cancel() may leak
rules.note: New context with cancel detected. Verify defer cancel() is present on the next line.

rules.description: Maps used as caches or accumulators shall have eviction or size limits
rules.note: New map allocation. If this map accumulates unbounded data (caches, request counters), ensure it has eviction or size limits.

rules.description: HTTP calls shall use context with timeout, not bare http.Get
rules.note: Bare HTTP call without context timeout detected. Use http.NewRequestWithContext with a reasonable timeout. Bare calls can block indefinitely.

rules.description: Encryption shall use pkg/crypto (AES-256-GCM), not raw crypto/aes calls
rules.note: Raw encryption call detected. Use pkg/crypto which provides AES-256-GCM with proper key management and envelope encryption. Raw crypto/aes usage may miss key rotation, IV handling, or authenticated encryption.

rules.description: Go code shall pass gosec security scan
rules.note: gosec found issues. Review findings and fix high/critical items. Install gosec: go install github.com/securecodewarrior/gosec/v2/cmd/gosec@latest

rules.description: UI components shall pass axe-core accessibility checks
rules.command: npx axe-cli --exit --stdout 2>/dev/null || echo 'axe-cli not installed — install via: npm i -g @axe-core/cli'
rules.note: Accessibility violations detected. Review axe output. Key checks: color contrast, ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, heading hierarchy.

rules.description: Interactive elements shall have accessible labels
rules.note: Interactive element without aria-label or data-testid. Add an accessible label so screen readers can describe the control.

rules.description: Colour shall not be the only means of conveying information
rules.note: Colour-only status indicator. WCAG 1.4.1: ensure an additional indicator (icon, text, sr-only label) accompanies the colour.

rules.description: No function shall exceed 80 lines
rules.command: echo 'Install gocyclo: go install github.com/fzipp/gocyclo/cmd/gocyclo@latest'; gocyclo -over 15 {files} 2>/dev/null || echo 'gocyclo not installed'
rules.note: Large functions are hard to understand, test, and modify. Decompose into smaller single-responsibility functions. gocyclo -over 15 flags functions with cyclomatic complexity > 15.

rules.description: No function shall exceed 80 lines in TypeScript
rules.command: npx eslint --rule 'max-lines-per-function: [warn, {max: 80, skipBlankLines: true, skipComments: true}]' {files} 2>/dev/null || echo 'eslint not configured'
rules.note: Complex components should be decomposed. Consider extracting hooks, sub-components, or utility functions.

rules.description: Code shall be understandable to a new team member 12 months from now
rules.note: Run the maintainability LLM check to assess naming clarity, separation of concerns, self-documenting design, and extension surface.
